title: Jackson Family: Education and Retirement Planning
key_issues: Retirement income goal of $8,000 per month in today's dollars.
Funding for children's education at Eastern State University.
Saving for Brianna's wedding with a target of $15,000.
Current debt levels including mortgage and auto loans.
Insurance coverage adequacy for life and disability.
planning_issues: Need to assess retirement savings to meet income goals.
Plan for college funding for both children.
Evaluate current insurance policies for adequacy.
Manage existing debt and cash flow for future savings.
Estate planning needs to ensure proper asset distribution.
key_recommendations: Increase contributions to retirement accounts to maximize employer matches.
Establish a 529 plan for children's education funding.
Create a detailed budget to manage cash flow and reduce debt.
Review and update life insurance policies to ensure adequate coverage.
Consider additional estate planning measures beyond basic wills.
candidate_recommendations: Increase contributions to retirement accounts to maximize employer matches.
Establish a 529 plan for children's education funding.
Create a detailed budget to manage cash flow and reduce debt.
Review and update life insurance policies to ensure adequate coverage.
Consider additional estate planning measures beyond basic wills.
raw_text_excerpt: Jamie and Claire Jackson have asked for your help with a number of issues facing them as they begin to think about sending their children to university and someday retiring. They have both been in their current jobs since they moved to Ohio 10 years ago to be closer to Jamie’s family. Claire’s parents both died 12 years ago. They are both happy with their current employment and have no intention of changing jobs in the near future. Family Information Jamie is a regional store manager at Apex Groceries, Inc. with an annual gross earned income of $124,400 and net after-tax income of $81,342. Claire earns an annual gross earned income of $59,400 and net after-tax income of $42,310 from her job as an executive assistant at Boeing, Inc. Family Member Age Relationship Health Jamie 40 Husband Excellent – Nonsmoker Claire 38 Wife Excellent – Nonsmoker Brianna 14 Daughter Excellent – Nonsmoker Riley 12 Son Excellent – Nonsmoker Earned Income and Tax Information furnished by the Jackson’s Federal Income Tax Calculation: Based on Tax Year 2019 Wage Earner Gross Income FICA State Federal 401(k) Net Income Jamie $124,400 $9,516 $5,598 $19,236 $8,708 $81,342 Claire $59,400 $4,540 $2,670 $6,320 $3,560 $42,310 Total $183,800 $14,056 $8,268 $25,556 $12,268 $123,651 Goals and Objectives In order of priority, the specific goals and objectives of Jamie and Claire are as follows: 1. Retire with an income of $8,000 per month in today's dollars when Jamie turns 65, with funding for a projected 30-year life expectancy. 2. Education funding for the children. 3. Wedding for Brianna - save $15,000 by Brianna's age 25. The wedding fund savings will be invested 50% in conservative stocks and 50% in corporate bonds.
